notes: Examples teach safe xLights sequencing behavior and command graph structure.
Examples must not encode preferred artistic style, pacing, color taste, or prop bias.
Historical sequences may be mined for valid functionality patterns only.
Examples should teach capability usage and safety boundaries, not visual resemblance to prior user sequences.

examples.intent: Apply approved chorus energy increase on focal props using current handoff graph.
focus: use current plan_handoff_v1 when scope and base revision still match
preserve deterministic command ordering
apply via transaction-only flow
expectedBehavior: reuses handoff graph by default
validates capability and command graph before apply
expects post-apply readback verification

examples.intent: Revise a single section when analysis_handoff_v1 is unavailable.
focus: continue planning with reduced confidence
emit explicit degraded-mode warning
avoid inventing unsupported timing semantics
expectedBehavior: plan remains validation-ready
warnings include reduced-confidence notice
scope remains anchored to intent handoff

examples.intent: Apply only a subset of proposed lines for one target and one section.
focus: generate a fresh plan for partial scope
avoid blindly reusing full-scope handoff commands
keep command graph deterministic
expectedBehavior: partial-scope graph contains only the requested section/target impact
validation-ready output is preserved
summary/metadata reflects narrowed scope

examples.intent: User manually adjusted timing track contents and now requests a new sequence revision.
focus: treat current timing tracks as cumulative sequence state
do not auto-lock timing tracks on prior manual edits
plan from latest revision
expectedBehavior: timing writes remain allowed unless policy explicitly disables them
revision freshness gates prevent stale overwrite
agent may update timing tracks again as part of new revision

examples.intent: Distribute activity across a group, but preserve xLights-native group render semantics unless the request explicitly forces member-level expansion.
focus: treat non-default group buffer/render policies as meaningful sequencing semantics
preserve overlay, stack, single-line, and per-model-strand group targets unless a stronger override is present
allow lower-risk per-model policies to expand with explicit per-member phrasing
expectedBehavior: soft member-distribution phrases preserve the group render target and emit a warning when semantics would likely be lost
high-risk group policies require force-style member expansion language before flattening or direct-member fanout
success is based on semantic correctness, not on maximizing distribution by default

examples.intent: Refine a broad group bed with more specific focal effects and ensure the sequence-level render setting supports proper blending.
focus: treat supportsModelBlending as sequence-level rendering state, not layout state
enable model blending when broad group coverage is intentionally refined by more specific targets
do not mutate unrelated sequence settings
expectedBehavior: planner reads current sequenceSettings before deciding whether a mutation is needed
planner emits sequence.setSettings only when supportsModelBlending must change to preserve intended layered rendering
effect commands depend on the sequence settings mutation when one is emitted

examples.intent: Sequence changed in xLights after plan generation and before apply.
focus: fail closed on revision mismatch
classify blocked apply correctly
require regenerate against latest state
expectedBehavior: apply_result_v1 reports blocked status
failure reason classifies revision conflict
stale plan is not applied
